title: Installing Uniface
text: Installing Uniface The installation program copies the directories, files, and executables required to run Rocket® Uniface, and configures them to run out-of-the box. Note: When installing Uniface 10 for the first time, always install it into a clean directory or library . For the Rocket® Uniface Enterprise Edition, do not install over an existing Uniface installation. Uniface supports many different platforms, but not all products and functionality are available on each platform, so check the installation requirements for each product. The products installed depend on the Uniface edition and platform that you have. For details on supported platforms and products, see the online Platform Availability Matrix . After installation, you can change the default configuration to suit your environment. For more information, see Configuring Uniface and Uniface Applications . For information on configuring database and network connectors, and other integration support, see Database Connectors and Support for Distributed Applications , and your database documentation. For products that work with Uniface, such as databases, web servers, and so on, consult your database and third-party product documentation. For other products supplied with Uniface, such as Uniface DLM, consult the instructions provided in the Uniface distribution. The readme file contains additional information about installation and configuration. This information takes precedence over the current documentation. Related Links Configuring Uniface and Uniface Applications Deploying a Uniface enterprise application is largely a matter of configuring the environment to ensure that all components are running in the appropriate locations and that Uniface is able to find them. Configuration files ensure that the Uniface runtime environment can locate and initialize the application components correctly. Database Connectors To access, retrieve, and store data, Uniface provides database connectors that translate Uniface I/O requests into the target database's data definition and manipulation languages. TCP Connector The TCP connector provides network connectivity via the TCP/IP protocol. It handles unsecured IPv4 and IPv6 connections. It also establishes the physical connection for secure communication using the TLS connector. TLS Connector The TLS network connector provides an encrypted network connection between the Uniface Router and its Uniface Servers and client applications. It can also be used by the UPOPMAIL component to connect to SMTP servers over TLS. Integrating with Other Technologies The Uniface architecture enables you to integrate Uniface and non-Uniface components in a single application, and to deploy the application on a large variety of platforms and technologies.
